title: houstonia triflora
intro: Houstonia triflora, also known as the dwarf bluet or low blue bluet, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to North America and is often found in wetlands and along streams. The medicinal properties of Houstonia triflora include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. The plant can be prepared as a tincture or tea, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system.
